content: Swift Foundation Process pipe-draining: use separate Pipe() for stdout and stderr, set readabilityHandler on each fileHandleForReading using availableData. Empty data signals EOF (pipe close). Both handlers run on background threads dispatched by Foundation. This is safe for concurrent reads. Do NOT use readDataToEndOfFile() as it blocks indefinitely. Set both handlers before calling process.launch()/run(). The readabilityHandler is NOT called on Linux EOF reliably (Darwin/macOS is fine). For this macOS-only app readabilityHandler + availableData pattern is canonical.
